canonical_wavefunction.description: Standard hydrogen wavefunction in spherical coordinates (m=0 restriction applied; see choice_points CP-1)
canonical_wavefunction.source: Griffiths, Introduction to Quantum Mechanics, 3rd ed., eq. 4.73 (Cambridge 2018)

radial_wavefunction_explicit.description: Normalized radial eigenfunction for hydrogen (principal quantum number n, angular momentum quantum number l)
radial_wavefunction_explicit.latex: R_{n\ell}(r) = \sqrt{\left(\frac{2}{n a_0}\right)^3 \frac{(n-\ell-1)!}{2n\,[(n+\ell)!]^3}} \, e^{-r/(n a_0)} \left(\frac{2r}{n a_0}\right)^\ell L_{n-\ell-1}^{2\ell+1}\!\left(\frac{2r}{n a_0}\right)

spherical_harmonic_m0.note: P_l is the Legendre polynomial of degree l

spiral_parameter_from_r.description: Spiral parameter theta as function of radial coordinate r (from P-2, P-3, prism_formal_spec.md §4.2)
spiral_parameter_from_r.notes: Defined for all r > 0; theta -> -infinity as r -> 0 (inner winding); theta -> +infinity as r -> infinity

r_from_spiral_parameter.description: Radial coordinate as function of spiral parameter (forward embedding map, P-2, P-3)

radial_in_spiral_coords.description: Radial wavefunction expressed in spiral parameter theta (substituting r = r_0 exp(alpha theta))
radial_in_spiral_coords.latex: R_{n\ell}^{(\mathrm{spiral})}(\theta) = C_{n\ell} \, e^{\alpha \ell \theta} \, \exp\!\left(-\frac{r_0 e^{\alpha\theta}}{n a_0}\right) L_{n-\ell-1}^{2\ell+1}\!\left(\frac{2 r_0 e^{\alpha\theta}}{n a_0}\right)

normalization_prefactor.description: C_nl collects the numerical prefactor after substituting r = r_0 exp(alpha theta)

radial_in_spiral_coords.note: r_0 (spiral reference radius) is not yet fixed by P-1..P-6; it scales normalization but not n-dependence. Will be set in P1-T4.

chi_coupling_factor.description: Chi-coupling factor f(chi, theta) implementing P-4 (chi = theta mod L_chi); see choice_points CP-2
chi_coupling_factor.justification: P-4 is a deterministic map; unique probability density implementing it without free parameters is the delta function

lifted_wavefunction_5D.description: 5D wavefunction on M5 (PRISM lift of psi_nl; Step 1C output)

components.radial_spiral: R_nl^(spiral)(theta(r)) — radial part in spiral coords
components.angular: Y_l^0(theta_pol) — standard spherical harmonic at m=0
components.chi_localization: delta(chi - theta(r) mod L_chi) — P-4 coupling

jacobian_from_formal_spec.description: Jacobian of 5D embedding map (from P-6 and prism_formal_spec.md §4.2, derived from P-2, P-3, P-4)
jacobian_from_formal_spec.note: The exp(3 alpha theta) factor concentrates at small r (theta -> -inf) and drives s-state enhancement for P1-T3

compactification_length.description: Circumference of compact chi dimension (P-5, derived from closure condition P-2+P-3+P-4)

projection_operator.description: Operator P: L^2(M5) -> L^2(R^3) that recovers psi_nl from Psi_nl (Step 1D; evaluated in Step 2)
projection_operator.note: Chi-integral fires exactly once (delta function in [0, L_chi)). R_nl^(spiral)(theta(r)) = R_nl(r) by back-substitution. N_lift = 1.
projection_operator.dimensional_check: chi is dimensionless (radian); d(chi) is dimensionless; Psi_nl ~ [length]^{-3/2}; result ~ [length]^{-3/2} as required for psi_nl

chi_integral_result.description: Result of the chi projection integral (Step 2.1)
chi_integral_result.explanation: Delta function argument theta(r) mod L_chi lies in [0, L_chi) by definition. Delta fires exactly once in the integration domain. Integral = 1.

jacobian_factor_coordinate_change.description: Jacobian factor from change of variable r <-> theta (Step 2.2). Enters P1-T3 energy integral.
jacobian_factor_coordinate_change.derivation: From r = r_0 exp(alpha theta), differentiating: dr = alpha r d(theta), so d(theta)/dr = 1/(alpha r)
jacobian_factor_coordinate_change.significance: This is the non-trivial factor produced by the r<->chi coupling. It appears in the 5D->4D measure conversion and in the Hamiltonian matrix element integral for Delta_E_nS in P1-T3.

measure_conversion_check.description: Verification that 5D spiral measure converts to standard 4D spherical measure (Step 2.2)
measure_conversion_check.derivation: J d(theta) = alpha r_0^3 exp(3 alpha theta) sin(theta_pol) * dr/(alpha r) = r_0^3 exp(3 alpha theta)/r * sin(theta_pol) dr = r^3/r * sin(theta_pol) dr = r^2 sin(theta_pol) dr

N_lift.description: Normalization constant for the 5D wavefunction lift (Step 2.3 — FIXED)
N_lift.derivation: Impose 4D projected normalization (P-6 consistent): integral d^3r |P*Psi_nl|^2 = N_lift^2 * [int R_nl^2 r^2 dr] * [int |Y_l^0|^2 sin(theta) dOmega] = N_lift^2 * 1 * 1 = 1. Therefore N_lift = 1.
N_lift.convention_justification: P-6: 4D wavefunction is the physical observable; physical probabilities are 4D integrals. Direct 5D integration with delta^2 is distributional and requires a regulator that would introduce a free parameter (violating the no-new-parameter rule from P-1..P-6).

projected_wavefunction_final.description: Final form of the 4D-projected wavefunction after chi-integration (Step 2 output)
projected_wavefunction_final.note: Exactly recovers the canonical hydrogen wavefunction. The lift and projection are norm-preserving. N_lift = 1.

choice_points.description: m=0 restriction: work at magnetic quantum number m=0 because the chi-integral and J are azimuthally symmetric under P-2 (planar spiral). Matrix element for Delta_E_nS is same for all m at fixed (n,l).
Delta-function localization: f(chi, theta) = delta(chi - theta mod L_chi) is the unique choice consistent with P-4 that introduces no free parameter.
alternatives_rejected: Gaussian of width sigma_chi: requires free parameter sigma_chi — violates no-new-parameter rule
Flat 1/L_chi: incompatible with deterministic P-4 coupling

choice_points.description: Normalization convention: 4D projected norm vs. regulated 5D direct norm. Chose 4D projected norm (P-6 consistent; avoids distributional delta^2).
choice_points.note: Under either convention, the projected wavefunction equals psi_nl(r,theta_pol). N_lift = 1 under the 4D projected norm. Under a regulated 5D norm, N_lift would depend on the regulator but the projected wavefunction result is the same after the regulator is removed.

t_theta_assumption.assumed: t proportional to theta (linear; non-relativistic Schrodinger limit)
t_theta_assumption.status: documented assumed limit; not derived from P-1..P-6
t_theta_assumption.check_in: P1-T3 (when lifted Hamiltonian is written; if linearity fails to recover Schrodinger equation, that step will HALT)

step_2_summary.measure_conversion: J(theta, theta_pol) d(theta) = r^2 sin(theta_pol) dr [exact]

open_items_for_next_steps.item: Show angular momentum quantization (n, l integers) emerges from winding-count condition on compact chi dimension (spec §8 P1-T2 success criterion)
Fix r_0 in terms of a_0 (non-structural; affects absolute normalization but not n-dependence of Delta_E_nS)
Use Jacobian factor 1/(alpha r) in the Hamiltonian matrix element integral for Delta_E_nS. This is the load-bearing geometric factor from Step 2.

review_requested_from: Kip Madden (principal; per spec §11 theoretician human-in-the-loop requirement)
